title: When is a grilling question ready to leave the round?
summary: A question advances only when evidence and business context make it answerable; missing company knowledge is delegated without losing session state.

options.label: Delegate the context gap
options.description: Preserves the session, sends one complete teammate ask, then resumes with the answer.

options.label: Make a local assumption
options.description: Faster, but unsafe when the missing fact changes business behavior or ownership.

options.label: Pause the session
options.description: Avoids a bad assumption but loses momentum and makes handoff harder.

states.label: Draft
states.description: Question exists but may still hide assumptions

states.label: Evidence seeking
states.description: Repo, docs, and current behavior are being checked

states.label: Answerable
states.description: Context and choices are clear enough for the owner
states.detail: The recommendation explains technical and business consequences.

states.label: Context blocked
states.description: A required company fact is unavailable locally

states.label: Delegated
states.description: A teammate ask packet is waiting for an answer

states.label: Resolved
states.description: The answer is recorded in the decision log

transitions.description: Inspect the repository and relevant documentation.
Package context, recommendation, question, and visual.
Resume the same session with the new fact.
